title: Scaffolders, stagers and riggers
description: Directly supervise and coordinate activities of construction or extraction workers. Direct work crews installing residential or commercial solar photovoltaic or thermal systems. Position and secure steel bars or mesh in concrete forms in order to reinforce concrete. Use a variety of fasteners, rod-bending machines, blowtorches, and hand tools. Includes rod busters. (Based on 24 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Signal or verbally direct workers engaged in hoisting and moving loads to ensure safety of workers and materials. Clean and maintain tools and equipment. Operate machines or equipment to remove, package, store, or transport loads of waste materials. Identify asbestos, lead, or other hazardous materials to be removed, using monitoring devices. Inspect buildings to identify required weatherization measures, including repair work, modification, or replacement. Prepare cost estimates or specifications for rehabilitation or weatherization services. Install and seal air ducts, combustion air openings, or ventilation openings to improve heating and cooling efficiency. Install photovoltaic (PV) systems in accordance with codes and standards, using drawings, schematics, and instructions.

Prepare hazardous material for removal or storage. Comply with prescribed safety procedures or federal laws regulating waste disposal methods. Build containment areas prior to beginning abatement or decontamination work. Remove high spots and cut bevels, using hand files, portable grinders, and cutting torches. Dismantle structures or equipment. Clean contaminated equipment or areas for reuse, using detergents or solvents, sandblasters, filter pumps, or steam cleaners. Remove asbestos or lead from surfaces, using hand or power tools such as scrapers, vacuums, or high-pressure sprayers.

Prepare or assist in the preparation of bids, contracts, or written reports related to weatherization work. Maintain activity logs, financial transaction logs, or other records of weatherization work performed. Contact residents or building owners to schedule appointments. Explain recommendations, policies, procedures, requirements, or other related information to residents or building owners. Discuss fencing needs with customers, and estimate and quote prices.

Wrap air ducts and water lines with insulating materials, such as duct wrap and pipe insulation. Apply insulation materials, such as loose, blanket, board, and foam insulation to attics, crawl spaces, basements, or walls.

Wrap water heaters with water heater blankets. Make minor repairs using basic hand or power tools and materials, such as glass, lumber, and drywall. Install storm windows or storm doors and verify proper fit. Test rigging to ensure safety and reliability. Install hot water tanks in accordance with building codes, manufacturer's specifications, and blueprints.

Skip non-relevant tasks that deal with bioremediation, hazardous waste management, or e-waste processing, then focus on construction-related tasks without losing relevant examples. For example, use tasks that focus on establishing locations for hoisting equipment or tasks related to handling structural steel.

Perform routine photovoltaic (PV) system maintenance on modules, arrays, batteries, power conditioning equipment, safety systems, structural systems, weather sealing, or balance of systems equipment. Activate photovoltaic (PV) systems to verify system functionality and conformity to performance expectations. Check electrical installation for proper wiring, polarity, grounding, or integrity of terminations. Diagram layouts and locations for photovoltaic (PV) arrays and equipment, including existing building or site features. Identify electrical, environmental, and safety hazards associated with photovoltaic (PV) installations.

Install photovoltaic (PV) systems in accordance with codes and standards, using drawings, schematics, and instructions. Assemble solar modules, panels, or support structures, as specified. Determine photovoltaic (PV) system designs or configurations based on factors such as customer needs, expectations, and site conditions. Determine appropriate sizes, ratings, and locations for all system overcurrent devices, disconnect devices, grounding equipment, and surge suppression equipment. Select gear, such as cables, pulleys, and winches, according to load weights and sizes, facilities, and work schedules.

Move and set up mobile homes or prefabricated buildings on owners' lots or at mobile home parks. Unload and position prefabricated steel units for hoisting, as needed. Hoist steel beams, girders, or columns into place, using cranes or signaling hoisting equipment operators to lift and position structural steel members. Fabricate, set up, and repair rigging, supporting structures, hoists, and pulling gear, using hand and power tools. Cut, bend, or weld steel pieces, using metal shears, torches, or welding equipment.

Remove damaged exterior panels, repair and replace structural frame members, and seal leaks, using hand tools. Clean and dress machine surfaces and component parts. Align, level, and anchor machinery. Lift or move materials and finished products, using large cranes. Cut rods to required lengths, using metal shears, hacksaws, bar cutters, or acetylene torches. Pull, push, or pry structural steel members into approximate positions for bolting into place. Attach loads to rigging to provide support or prepare them for moving, using hand and power tools.

Dismantle and store rigging equipment after use. Locate and repair frayed wiring, broken connections, or incorrect wiring, using ohmmeters, soldering irons, tape, and hand tools. Bolt aligned structural steel members in position for permanent riveting, bolting, or welding into place. Verify conformance of workpieces to specifications, using squares, rulers, and measuring tapes. Attach pulleys and blocks to fixed overhead structures, such as beams, ceilings, and gin pole booms, using bolts and clamps.

Remove or limit contamination following emergencies involving hazardous substances. Sort specialized hazardous waste at landfills or disposal centers, following proper disposal procedures. Clean mold-contaminated sites by removing damaged porous materials or thoroughly cleaning all contaminated nonporous materials. Drive trucks or other heavy equipment to convey contaminated waste to designated sea or ground locations. Test combustible appliances, such as gas appliances. Connect water hoses to inlet pipes of plumbing systems, and test operation of plumbing fixtures.

Examine designs to determine requirements for all parts of the photovoltaic (PV) system electrical circuit, identify installation locations with proper orientation and structural integrity for photovoltaic (PV) arrays, and assess potential solar installation sites to determine feasibility and design requirements.
Identify and resolve deficiencies in photovoltaic (PV) system installation or materials, and analyze worker or production problems recommending solutions to improve installation efficiency.

Identify methods for laying out, orienting, and mounting modules or arrays to ensure efficient installation and system maintenance, and suggest cost-reduction strategies for solar installation projects.

Install module array interconnect wiring, implementing measures to disable arrays during installation; install required labels on solar system components and hardware; and prepare solar installation project proposals, quotes, budgets, or schedules.
Measure and analyze system performance and operating parameters to assess operating condition, test operating voltages, and monitor system functionality including start-up and shut-down operations.
Program, adjust, or configure inverters and controls for desired set points and operating modes; and ensure compliance with codes during installations of photovoltaic (PV) solar systems.
Visually inspect and test photovoltaic (PV) modules or systems, and compile or maintain records of system operation, performance, and maintenance.

Supervise solar installers, technicians, and subcontractors for solar installation projects to ensure compliance with safety standards; coordinate or schedule building inspections; and monitor work of contractors to ensure conformity to plans and specifications.

Provide technical assistance to installers and technicians in areas such as installation methods, safety procedures, and equipment operation, and train workers in construction methods and operation of equipment.
Assess and coordinate work activities with other construction project activities, and confer with managerial or technical personnel to resolve problems or coordinate activities.
Arrange for equipment repairs, order materials or supplies, and maintain necessary operational data and reports on personnel and production.
Evaluate subcontractors or subcontractor bids for quality, cost, and reliability; and estimate materials, equipment, and personnel needed for solar installation projects.

Select mechanical designs, installation equipment, or installation plans that conform to environmental, architectural, structural, and code requirements; and deduce proper installation sequencing for maximized efficiency.

Locate, measure, and mark site locations or placement of structures or equipment, using appropriate measuring and marking equipment; inspect work progress, equipment, or construction sites to ensure safety and compliance with specifications.
Clean work areas and ensure equipment or workspaces are in proper operational condition as per company policies.

Confer with engineering or supervisory personnel to exchange technical information and determine methods or sequences needed to fabricate products; study blueprints or work orders to assess project specifications.
Perform start-up of systems for testing or customer implementation; monitor operational efficiency, and record production output related to equipment and performance evaluation.

Inspect or measure finished workpieces to determine conformance to specifications; make minor electrical and mechanical repairs and adjustments to machines; and notify supervisors for major service requirements.
Activate machine start-up switches to engage operational settings, and make necessary adjustments to resolve operational issues within machining tasks.

Lift workpieces to machines manually or with hoists or cranes, position and secure them using fasteners and hand tools, and maintain machines and remove or replace broken or worn machine tools, using hand tools.

Start machines, monitor their operations, record operational data, troubleshoot, maintain, and make minor repairs to equipment, and clean machines, tooling, or parts, using solvents or solutions and rags.

Position and align workpieces against fixtures or stops on machine beds or on dies, and position, align, and secure cutting tools in toolholders on machines, using hand tools, while verifying their positions with measuring instruments.
Examine completed workpieces for defects, measure dimensions to ensure conformance to specifications, and sort defective pieces according to types of flaws.

Set up, operate, or tend machines to perform various operations on metal or plastic materials, such as cutting, shaping, or assembling parts, using knowledge of shop mathematics and tooling instructions.

Adjust machine controls and change tool settings to keep dimensions within specified tolerances, and study blueprints, layouts or charts, and job orders for information on specifications and tooling instructions.
Signal and assist other workers to remove and position equipment, fill hoppers, and feed materials into machines.
Clean work areas and maintain an inventory of materials.
Direct and train other workers to change rolls, operate mill equipment, remove coils and cobbles, and band and load material.
Measure and examine extruded products to locate defects and to check for conformance to specifications, adjusting controls as necessary to alter products.
Read work orders or production schedules to determine specifications, such as materials to be used, locations of cutting lines, or dimensions and tolerances.
